title: There's a Lesson in That Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Not allowed by law or rules
choices: illegal
entry
supplied
rounds.answer: illegal
rounds.explanation: illegal: Not allowed by law or rules

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A member of a strict English Protestant group from the 16th and 17th centuries
choices: illegal
entry
Puritan
rounds.answer: Puritan
rounds.explanation: Puritan: A member of a strict English Protestant group from the 16th and 17th centuries

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The official approval of something, like a law or agreement
choices: entry
ratification
illegal
rounds.answer: ratification
rounds.explanation: ratification: The official approval of something, like a law or agreement

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A person who follows the teachings of Jesus Christ
rounds.answer: christian

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The activity of fighting in a war
rounds.answer: warfare

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A room or space used for a special purpose
rounds.answer: chamber

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: It is illegal to steal someone else's property.
The teacher supplied us with new books for our class.
The Puritans believed in living a simple, strict religious life.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: A big change in the form or shape of something
A member of a strict English Protestant group from the 16th and 17th centuries
A way of looking at or thinking about something
The activity of fighting in a war
rounds.answer: A way of looking at or thinking about something
rounds.explanation: Everyone has a different perspective on what makes a good story.

choices: A big change in the form or shape of something
A member of a strict English Protestant group from the 16th and 17th centuries
A person chosen to help decide a court case
To remove or hide parts of something considered inappropriate
rounds.answer: To remove or hide parts of something considered inappropriate
rounds.explanation: The teacher had to censor some parts of the film before showing it.

choices: A big change in the form or shape of something
A person chosen to help decide a court case
A person who creates something new
The activity of fighting in a war
rounds.answer: The activity of fighting in a war
rounds.explanation: The soldiers trained hard for the warfare they would face.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1: Retell a Fable from Memory
rounds.target: It is illegal to steal someone else's property.
The teacher supplied us with new books for our class.
The Puritans believed in living a simple, strict religious life.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The battery has a high voltage to power the toy car.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The emancipation of the slaves meant they could live freely.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The confederation of states helped them cooperate during difficult times.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'juror' mean in this unit?
choices: A person chosen to help decide a court case
Not allowed by law or rules
The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
rounds.answer: A person chosen to help decide a court case
rounds.explanation: juror: A person chosen to help decide a court case

rounds.prompt: What does 'nationalism' mean in this unit?
choices: Not allowed by law or rules
The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
The belief that your country is important and worth being proud of
rounds.answer: The belief that your country is important and worth being proud of
rounds.explanation: nationalism: The belief that your country is important and worth being proud of

rounds.prompt: What does 'entry' mean in this unit?
choices: Gave or provided something that was needed
The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
Not allowed by law or rules
rounds.answer: The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
rounds.explanation: entry: The act of going into a place, or the way to get in
